title: Agent Identity
description: Identity token for an AI agent making calls to OpenTrust-registered tools. Agents present this as a signed JWT in the X-OpenTrust-Agent-Identity header. Tools with caller_requirements.require_agent_identity=true will reject calls that lack a valid token.

agent_id.description: Globally unique identifier for this agent instance. Format: '{registry_domain}/{org_slug}/{agent_slug}'. Example: 'opentrust.dev/acme-corp/billing-agent'.

agent_type.description: autonomous: agent acts without per-action human approval. supervised: a human reviews outputs but not each action. human_in_the_loop: a human approves each significant action. Tools may require stricter types for sensitive operations.

operator.description: The person or organization operating this agent.

identity_value.description: The verified identity — GitHub username, org name, domain, or platform account ID.

trust_status.description: Trust level of this agent identity. Tools use this against caller_requirements.min_agent_trust_status.

spend_policy.description: The spend policy this agent is operating under. Embedded here so tools can verify the agent has been authorized to spend before processing a payment.

issued_at.description: When this identity token was issued.

expires_at.description: When this token expires. Agents should refresh tokens before expiry. Tools must reject expired tokens.

session_id.description: Optional session identifier. Allows tools to correlate multiple calls within one agent task.

spawned_by.description: agent_id of the parent agent that spawned this sub-agent. Null/absent for root agents. Allows tools to know they are being called inside an orchestration chain and trace accountability back to the root operator.

call_chain.description: Ordered list of agent_ids from the root agent to this agent. Example: ['root-agent-id', 'sub-agent-a-id', 'this-agent-id']. Tools use this for cycle detection (reject if their own slug appears in the chain) and depth enforcement. The root operator in call_chain[0]'s identity token is financially responsible for all costs.

depth.description: Current orchestration depth. 0 = root agent. Increments by 1 for each spawning level. Tools may reject calls above their caller_requirements max depth.

policy_url.description: Optional URL to a signed spend policy document. Use this instead of embedding the full spend_policy object when the policy is large (e.g., dynamic_budget_allocation with many entries) and would push the JWT past typical HTTP header limits (~8KB). The URL must serve a JSON document matching spend-policy.schema.json, and the response must include an X-OpenTrust-Signature header so the receiver can verify authenticity. When policy_url is present and spend_policy is absent, tools must fetch and verify the policy before enforcing it. Caching is permitted for the duration of the token's validity window.

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over the canonical token payload. The operator's key must be registered with an OpenTrust registry. If this key_id appears in the operator key revocation list at https://opentrust.dev/.well-known/revoked-operator-keys.json, the token must be rejected immediately regardless of signature validity.

key_id.description: The operator's registered key ID. Format: '{operator_identity_value}-{year}-{n}'. Agents look this up in the registry's operator key set and check it against the revocation list before verifying the signature.

algorithm.description: Ed25519 is the only accepted algorithm. Agents must reject tokens with any other algorithm value, including 'none'.

value.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over SHA-256(canonical_token_json) where canonical_token_json has keys sorted, whitespace stripped, and the signature field excluded.
